documentation.value:
```swift
let name: Type = value
```
Declares an immutable constant.

```swift
var name: Type = value
```
Declares a mutable variable.

```swift
enum Name {
    case value1
    case value2
}
```
Declares an enumeration type.

```swift
import Foundation
```
Imports a module.

```swift
return value
```
Returns a value from a function.

```swift
case .value:
    // handler
```
Switch case or enum case.

```swift
default:
    break
```
Default case in switch statement.
Exits the current loop or switch case.
Skips the current iteration and continues with the next.
Causes execution to fall through to the next case in a switch statement.

```swift
throw MyError.someError
```
Throws an error.
Marks a function that can throw errors.
Marks a function that throws only if its closure argument throws.

```swift
try expression
try? expression
try! expression
```
Attempts a throwing expression.
Type cast operator. Use `as`, `as?`, or `as!`.
Checks if a value is of a certain type.
Used in for-in loops and closure parameters.
Adds constraints to generic types or pattern matching.
Refers to the current instance.
Refers to the current type in protocol or class context.
Refers to the superclass.

```swift
typealias Name = ExistingType
```
Creates a named alias for an existing type.

```swift
associatedtype Element
```
Declares an associated type in a protocol.
Declares a type-level (static) member.
Prevents a class, method, or property from being overridden.

```swift
lazy var name: Type = expression
```
Delays initialization until first access.

```swift
weak var delegate: Protocol?
```
Creates a weak reference to avoid retain cycles.
Creates an unowned reference that is not optional.
Marks a protocol requirement as optional (requires @objc).
Requires all subclasses to implement this initializer.
Allows a method to modify value type properties.
Indicates a setter that does not modify the instance.
Overrides a superclass method or property.
Allows access and overriding outside the module.
Allows access outside the module but not overriding.
Default access level; accessible within the module.
Restricts access to the enclosing file.
Restricts access to the enclosing declaration.
Passes a parameter by reference so it can be modified.

```swift
func make() -> some View { ... }
```
Declares an opaque return type.

```swift
var value: any Protocol
```
Declares an existential type.

```swift
func fetchData() async -> Data { ... }
```
Marks a function as asynchronous.

```swift
let result = await fetchData()
```
Suspends execution until async call completes.
Opts a member out of actor isolation.
Specifies the actor isolation for a parameter.
Transfers ownership of the parameter to the function.
Borrows the parameter without taking ownership.

```swift
@Published var value: Type
```
Publishes changes for Combine subscribers.

```swift
@State private var value: Type
```
Manages local state in SwiftUI views.

```swift
@Binding var value: Type
```
Creates a two-way connection to a state value.

```swift
func print(_ items: Any..., separator: String = " ", terminator: String = "\n")
```
Writes textual representations to standard output.

```swift
func debugPrint(_ items: Any..., separator: String = " ", terminator: String = "\n")
```
Writes debug textual representations to standard output.

```swift
func dump<T>(_ value: T, name: String? = nil, indent: Int = 0, maxDepth: Int = .max, maxItems: Int = .max) -> T
```
Dumps an object's contents using its mirror.

```swift
func min<T: Comparable>(_ x: T, _ y: T) -> T
```
Returns the lesser of two comparable values.

```swift
func max<T: Comparable>(_ x: T, _ y: T) -> T
```
Returns the greater of two comparable values.

```swift
func abs<T: Comparable & SignedNumeric>(_ x: T) -> T
```
Returns the absolute value of the given number.

```swift
func stride<T>(from: T, to: T, by: T.Stride) -> StrideTo<T>
```
Returns a sequence from start to (but not including) end.

```swift
func zip<Sequence1, Sequence2>(_ sequence1: Sequence1, _ sequence2: Sequence2) -> Zip2Sequence<Sequence1, Sequence2>
```
Creates a sequence of pairs from two sequences.

```swift
func map<T>(_ transform: (Element) throws -> T) rethrows -> [T]
```
Returns an array containing results of mapping the given closure.

```swift
func filter(_ isIncluded: (Element) throws -> Bool) rethrows -> [Element]
```
Returns elements satisfying the given predicate.

```swift
func reduce<Result>(_ initialResult: Result, _ nextPartialResult: (Result, Element) throws -> Result) rethrows -> Result
```
Combines all elements using the given closure.

```swift
func compactMap<T>(_ transform: (Element) throws -> T?) rethrows -> [T]
```
Returns non-nil results of mapping the given closure.

```swift
func flatMap<T: Sequence>(_ transform: (Element) throws -> T) rethrows -> [T.Element]
```
Returns flattened results of mapping the given closure.

```swift
func sorted() -> [Element]
func sorted(by: (Element, Element) throws -> Bool) rethrows -> [Element]
```
Returns the elements sorted.

```swift
func forEach(_ body: (Element) throws -> Void) rethrows
```
Calls the given closure on each element.

```swift
func contains(_ element: Element) -> Bool
func contains(where: (Element) throws -> Bool) rethrows -> Bool
```
Returns whether the sequence contains the element.

```swift
var first: Element?
```
The first element of the collection, or nil if empty.

```swift
var last: Element?
```
The last element of the collection, or nil if empty.

```swift
var count: Int
```
The number of elements in the collection.

```swift
var isEmpty: Bool
```
A Boolean value indicating whether the collection is empty.

```swift
mutating func append(_ newElement: Element)
```
Adds an element to the end of the array.

```swift
mutating func insert(_ newElement: Element, at i: Int)
```
Inserts an element at the specified position.

```swift
mutating func remove(at index: Int) -> Element
```
Removes and returns the element at the specified index.

```swift
mutating func removeAll(keepingCapacity: Bool = false)
```
Removes all elements from the collection.

```swift
func enumerated() -> EnumeratedSequence<Self>
```
Returns pairs of (offset, element).

```swift
func prefix(_ maxLength: Int) -> Slice<Self>
```
Returns the first maxLength elements.

```swift
func suffix(_ maxLength: Int) -> Slice<Self>
```
Returns the last maxLength elements.

```swift
func joined(separator: String = "") -> String
```
Joins string elements with a separator.

```swift
func split(separator: Character, maxSplits: Int = .max, omittingEmptySubsequences: Bool = true) -> [Substring]
```
Splits a string by the given separator.

```swift
func replacingOccurrences(of target: String, with replacement: String) -> String
```
Returns a new string replacing all occurrences of target.

```swift
func hasPrefix(_ prefix: String) -> Bool
```
Returns whether the string begins with the specified prefix.

```swift
func hasSuffix(_ suffix: String) -> Bool
```
Returns whether the string ends with the specified suffix.

```swift
func uppercased() -> String
```
Returns an uppercased version of the string.

```swift
func lowercased() -> String
```
Returns a lowercased version of the string.

```swift
func trimmingCharacters(in set: CharacterSet) -> String
```
Returns a string with characters in the given set trimmed.

```swift
struct Int: FixedWidthInteger, SignedInteger
```
A signed integer value type.

```swift
struct Double: FloatingPoint
```
A double-precision, floating-point value type.

```swift
struct Float: FloatingPoint
```
A single-precision, floating-point value type.

```swift
struct String: StringProtocol
```
A Unicode string value.

```swift
struct Bool
```
A value type whose instances are either true or false.

```swift
struct Array<Element>: RandomAccessCollection, MutableCollection
```
An ordered, random-access collection.

```swift
struct Dictionary<Key: Hashable, Value>: Collection
```
A collection of key-value pairs.

```swift
struct Set<Element: Hashable>: Collection
```
An unordered collection of unique elements.

```swift
enum Optional<Wrapped>: ExpressibleByNilLiteral
```
A type that represents either a wrapped value or nil.

```swift
enum Result<Success, Failure: Error>
```
A value that represents either a success or a failure.

```swift
struct Character
```
A single extended grapheme cluster.

```swift
struct UInt: FixedWidthInteger, UnsignedInteger
```
An unsigned integer value type.
An 8-bit signed integer type.
A 16-bit signed integer type.
A 32-bit signed integer type.
A 64-bit signed integer type.

```swift
struct Data: Collection
```
A byte buffer in memory.

```swift
struct URL: Hashable
```
A value that identifies the location of a resource.

```swift
struct Date: Comparable, Hashable
```
A specific point in time.

```swift
protocol Error
```
A type representing an error value that can be thrown.

```swift
typealias Codable = Decodable & Encodable
```
A type that can convert itself to/from external representations.

```swift
protocol Hashable: Equatable
```
A type that can be hashed into a Hasher to produce an integer hash value.

```swift
protocol Equatable
```
A type that can be compared for value equality.

```swift
protocol Comparable: Equatable
```
A type that can be compared using relational operators.

```swift
protocol Sendable
```
A type whose values can safely be passed across concurrency domains.

```swift
protocol AsyncSequence
```
A type providing asynchronous, sequential, iterated access to its elements.

```swift
struct Task<Success: Sendable, Failure: Error>
```
A unit of asynchronous work.

```swift
switch value {
case .case1:
    break
case .case2:
    break
}
```
Exhaustive switch on enum.

```swift
enum MyError: Error {
    case notFound
    case invalidInput(String)
}
```
Custom error type.
Protocol with extension providing default implementation.

```swift
typealias Void = ()
```
The empty tuple type, used for functions that return nothing.

```swift
enum Never
```
A type with no values; used for functions that never return.
The protocol to which all types implicitly conform.
The protocol to which all classes implicitly conform.

```swift
struct Range<Bound: Comparable>
```
A half-open interval from a lower bound up to, but not including, an upper bound.

```swift
struct ClosedRange<Bound: Comparable>
```
An interval from a lower bound up to, and including, an upper bound.
Boolean literal true.
Boolean literal false.
The absence of a value for optional types.

```swift
import Foundation
```

```swift
import Combine
```
Apple's reactive programming framework.

Apple's imperative UI framework for iOS/tvOS.
Exposes the declaration to Objective-C runtime.
Suppresses the unused result warning.

```swift
@available(iOS 15.0, macOS 12.0, *)
```

A type providing sequential, iterated access to its elements.
A sequence whose elements can be traversed multiple times and accessed by indexed subscript.
A type-erased hashable value.
A slice of a string. Shares storage with the original String.

A 64-bit unsigned integer type.
